title: System Response to Internal Conditions: The Zero-Input Response
summary: This page motivates the practical assumption M ≤ N for LTIC differential-equation models by explaining why M > N implies differentiating behavior, instability for some inputs, and amplification of high-frequency noise. It then introduces the decomposition of total response into zero-input and zero-state responses and begins Section 2.2 by defining the zero-input response as the solution of the homogeneous equation Q(D)y0(t) = 0.
